case_title: JACQUELINE HOLDEN VS. MAVENTUS GROUP INC.

docket_entries.description: SEPARATE STATEMENT OF FACTS UNDISPUTED FACTS IN SUPPORT OF ITS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, AS TO PLAINTIFF JACQUELINE HOLDENS UNVERIFIED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100112219) FILED BY DEFENDANT MAVENTUS GROUP INC.

DECLARATION OF JACQUELINE HOLDEN IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF?S OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT?S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, AS TO PLAINTIFF JACQUELINE HOLDEN?S UNVERIFIED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 66219130) FILED BY PLAINTIFF HOLDEN, JACQUELINE , AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302 - DEFENDANT MAVENTUS GROUP, INC.'S SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION IS GRANTED. MAVENTUS HAS DEMONSTRATED THAT PLAINTIFF JACQUELINE HOLDEN NEVER PAID IT THE $170,000 WHICH WAS THE SUPPOSED CONSIDERATION FOR THE PROMISSORY NOTE (SSUF PP. 4, 7-18), AND HOLDEN'S OPPOSITION PAPERS MAKE NO ATTEMPT TO CLAIM OTHERWISE. THUS, HOLDEN'S CAUSE OF ACTION FOR "BREACH OF CONTRACT - PROMISSORY NOTE" FAILS FOR LACK OF CONSIDERATION. LIKEWISE, HOLDEN'S CONVERSION CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS, BECAUSE MAVENTUS DID NOT CONVERT, IMPROPERLY HOLD OR KEEP THE $170,000 FROM HER. HOLDEN'S DECLARATORY RELIEF CLAIM IS DERIVATIVE OF HER CONTRACT AND CONVERSION CLAIMS. MAVENTUS' OTHER SUMMARY JUDGMENT GROUNDS NEED NOT BE REACHED. AMENDMENT OF THE COMPLAINT WOULD BE FUTILE. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM.

NO APPEARANCES, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF JACQUELINE HOLDEN MOVES FOR A NEW TRIAL AFTER LOSING A SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION. THE NEW TRIAL MOTION, WHICH MAKES THREE ARGUMENTS, IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES OR ORDER SIGNED THIS DATE FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR NO. 5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Maventus Group Inc. For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar May-19-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Bao Pham, Esq. of Bohm wildish & Matsen LLP, 714.384.6500, for Plaintiff;
Atty. Denis Schmidt, Esq. of Orsus Gate LLP. 415.326.3558, for Defendant.

DEFENDANT MAVENTUS GROUP INC.'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES.

The matter is called. ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Maventus Group, Inc.'s motion for $73,125 in attorney fees and $1,915.91 in costs is granted. Plaintiff's opposition makes three arguments, all unavailing.

First, it is asserted that fees should be reduced because the case was "not complex" and the record not "voluminous." This was due in large part to the judicious way in which Maventus counsel defensed plaintiff's case. A total of $73,125 for a year-plus of litigation capped by a case dispositive summary judgment motion is reasonable.

Second, plaintiff complains that counsel worked "over 60 hours" on their summary judgment motion. Again, based on my 35 years as a litigator and judge, this was reasonable. (Serrano v. Priest (1977) 20 Cal.3d 25, 49 ("experienced trial judge is the best judge of the value of professional services rendered in his court").) Plaintiff cites an opinion from another jurisdiction that calculated fees per pages of briefing, but such an approach could only encourage prolixity.

Third, plaintiff says fees "should be reduced for hours that were block billed." No bills were adduced, so no "block billing" was shown. Defense counsel properly described their services and how many hours they worked in each aspect of the case. (See Syers Properties III, Inc. v. Rankin (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 691, 698-99.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

Motion For New Trial
Off Calendar May-07-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF JACQUELINE HOLDEN'S Motion For New Trial.

No appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Plaintiff Jacqueline Holden moves for a new trial after losing a summary judgment motion. The new trial motion, which makes three arguments, is denied.

First, Holden says "the court's denial of a continuance was an irregularity and abuse of discretion." In fact, I granted a continuance. After parting ways with her lawyer in mid-November 2020, Holden blew the deadline for opposing the summary judgment motion. I nonetheless continued the summary judgment hearing a month and allowed Holden a late opposition, which she filed.

Second, Holden claims "newly discovered evidence" - a purchase agreement. But Holden concedes she and her counsel possessed this document all along.

Third, Holden argues that summary judgment was improper because her opposition said "there are well over 20 disputed facts." A litigant's labeling a fact "disputed" does not make it so - or make it material. I considered both sides' evidence and found no triable issue of material fact.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

Motion For New Trial
Off Calendar Apr-28-2021 Continued To May-07-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JACQUELINE HOLDEN's Motion For New Trial.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Matter is continued to May 7, 2021 to be heard by Judge Ulmer. This new trial motion seeks review of an order granting summary judgment that Judge Ulmer issued.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-06-2021 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE (CONTINUED)
Off Calendar Jan-27-2021 Set For Case Management Conference On Apr-21-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication, As To Plaintiff Jacqueline Holdens Unverified Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-15-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances by ZOOM Video-conferencing:

Self-represented plaintiff, Jacqueline Holden (Tel: 415-961-09929) appears on her own behalf as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney Daniel Clasen appears briefly in favor of plaintiff but is dismissed as he is not counsel of record.

Attorney, Denis Shmidt (DShmidt@OrsusGate.com), of the law offices of ORSUS GATE LLP (Tel: 415-326-3558) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

On calendar for hearing:
DEFENDANT MAVENTUS GROUP INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication, As To Plaintiff Jacqueline Holdens Unverified Complaint

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides, on record as reflected in the notes of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Maventus Group, Inc.'s summary judgment motion is granted. Maventus has demonstrated that plaintiff Jacqueline Holden never paid it the $170,000 which was the supposed consideration for the promissory note (SSUF pp. 4, 7-18), and Holden's opposition papers make no attempt to claim otherwise. Thus, Holden's cause of action for "breach of contract - promissory note" fails for lack of consideration. Likewise, Holden's conversion cause of action fails, because Maventus did not convert, improperly hold or keep the $170,000 from her. Holden's declaratory relief claim is derivative of her contract and conversion claims. Maventus' other summary judgment grounds need not be reached. Amendment of the complaint would be futile.

Written order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com.
